Added Decision Point 0: gene set source check before pattern selection

key_strengths: Tumor/oncology exclusion boundary is now explicit and precise — colorectal cancer named as example eliminates scope ambiguity
Dataset-unavailability fallback prevents dead-end responses when GEO datasets are not yet identified, turning a gap into a useful search guide
Decision Point 0 (gene set source) surfaces mechanism gene quality as a first-class pre-step, improving reviewer-risk traceability
Borderline case note for cancer comorbidities demonstrates domain expertise and prevents over-refusal of legitimate non-tumor studies
Input 6 scope boundary score improved from 59 to 93 — the single largest improvement vector from v1 is now fully resolved

recommendations.title: Mid-Workflow Dataset Quality Escalation Path Missing
recommendations.problem: Dataset availability check now handles pre-plan no-data scenarios well. However, there is no guidance for a mid-workflow discovery: e.g., user identifies a dataset but it turns out to be too heterogeneous to batch-correct, or sample sizes after phenotype filtering are too small for the intended tier.
recommendations.root_cause: Fallback logic is placed at Step 1 entry only; no re-assessment checkpoint mid-plan.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief note to Step 4 (Generate Four Configurations): 'If dataset QC reveals platform heterogeneity or post-filtering n < 20 per group, re-evaluate tier selection. Downgrade to Lite or add a preliminary feasibility note to each tier.'

recommendations.title: Description Trigger List Could Cause False Triggers for Some Multi-Disease Studies
recommendations.problem: The description includes broad triggers like 'GEO datasets + machine learning' and 'immune infiltration + biomarker' without a non-tumor qualifier. A user with a mixed tumor/non-tumor co-morbidity study might trigger this skill unintentionally.
recommendations.root_cause: Trigger list in frontmatter description is optimized for recall but slightly under-specified for precision.
recommendations.fix: Append to description trigger list: 'Do not trigger for pure oncology or tumor studies — use a dedicated cancer ML skill for those.' This prevents false positives at the routing layer before the skill even runs.
